name: chem_vqe_diatomic_bench
description: Independent verification suite for qmirror chemistry-VQE — 10 well-studied small molecules cross-checked against published CCSD(T) reference values. Complements the CMT scaffolds (cmt_clc1/hd6/mfn2/sar1/gjb1) which are proprietary candidates. Per QMIRROR.md §10 + §18.

extraction_recipe.active_space_selection: frontier HOMO/LUMO around RHF reference; default (2,2)→4e/4o means 2 frozen core + 4 active electrons in 4 active spatial orbitals (= 8 spin-orbitals)
extraction_recipe.vqe_method: qiskit-nature UCCSD (reps=1) + SLSQP(maxiter=500, ftol=1e-9) from initial_point=zeros → converges << 1 µHa of CASCI(4,4)
vendored_outputs: UCCSD-converged statevector ψ* (2^n_qubits complex amplitudes — re + im arrays)

molecules.name: H2
molecules.ccsd_t_reference_kind: pyscf FCI runtime (= CCSD(T) for 2-electron singlet); = canonical H2/STO-3G/0.74 Å
molecules.ccsd_t_source: vendored from pyscf 2.13.0 RHF+FCI; matches qiskit-nature 0.7.2 N5 spec (Phase 10 absorption 2026-05-12)
molecules.notes: Reuses existing chemistry_vqe.hexa cond.14 N5 gate; trivially exact since UCCSD = CASCI(2,2) for 2 electrons.

molecules.name: LiH
molecules.ccsd_t_reference_kind: CASCI(4,4) vendored — equivalent to CCSD(T) within the (4e,4o) active space
molecules.ccsd_t_source: qiskit-nature 0.7.2 CASCI(4,4) at this geometry; literature CCSD(T)/cc-pVQZ(R=1.595 Å) is -8.0707 Ha (Hill 2013) — note basis/AS difference, the 1.6 mHa gate is vs the same-AS CASCI(4,4).
molecules.notes: Validation anchor — pre-existing 4e/4o module; bench harness wires it in directly.

molecules.name: LiH (4e/5o stretch)
molecules.notes: Stretch tier — full in-process Nelder-Mead via RFC 034+035 farr-handle path.

molecules.name: BeH2
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB experimental Re; CCSD(T) electronic energy from Karton & Martin, J. Chem. Phys. 136, 124114 (2012) (W4 reference set).
molecules.notes: Symmetric stretch; 6e total, freeze Be 1s → 4 active electrons. Classic textbook VQE benchmark (Kandala IBM 2017).

molecules.name: H2O
molecules.ccsd_t_reference_kind: CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVQZ at experimental equilibrium (Re=0.9572 Å, θ=104.52°)
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB; Tajti et al. (HEAT-345 protocol), J. Chem. Phys. 121, 11599 (2004). Total atomization-corrected best estimate -76.4376 Ha; CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVQZ tabulated.
molecules.notes: 10e total, freeze O 1s+2s+2p_z(bond plane orthogonal) → 4 active electrons. Bend angle from gas-phase microwave spectroscopy.

molecules.name: NH3
molecules.ccsd_t_reference_kind: CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVQZ at experimental equilibrium (Re=1.0124 Å, θ_HNH=106.67°)
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB; Klopper et al., Mol. Phys. 99, 481 (2001).
molecules.notes: 10e total, freeze N 1s + lone-pair-correlated orbitals → 4 active electrons in N-H σ frontier. Pyramidal C3v.

molecules.name: HF
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB; Feller, Peterson J. Chem. Phys. 110, 8384 (1999).
molecules.notes: 10e total. At STO-3G, HF has only 6 spatial orbitals (1 H + 5 F). Frozen-core inactive (3 orbitals × 2 = 6 electrons) leaves only 3 spatial orbitals for the active space — 4e/4o is geometrically infeasible. Requires a larger basis (6-31G adds polarization) OR a smaller active space (4e/3o or 2e/2o).

molecules.name: N2
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB; W4-17 reference set (Karton et al. 2017).
molecules.notes: 14e total; triple-bond → AS choice critical. Frontier (4e,4o) = π_x + π_y bonding/antibonding pair. Classic dissociation benchmark.

molecules.name: CO
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB; Feller & Peterson (W4 reference set).
molecules.notes: 14e total; iso-electronic to N2. (4e,4o) = π_x+π_y frontier (HOMO/LUMO bonding+antibonding).

molecules.name: F2
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB; W4-17 reference set (Karton et al. 2017).
molecules.notes: 18e total. At STO-3G, F2 has 10 spatial orbitals (5 per F). Frozen-core inactive (7 orbitals × 2 = 14 electrons) leaves only 3 spatial orbitals for the active space — 4e/4o is geometrically infeasible. Same shape as HF at STO-3G; needs a larger basis (cc-pVDZ+) for 4e/4o frontier.

molecules.name: CH4
molecules.ccsd_t_reference_kind: CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVQZ at experimental Re=1.0870 Å, Td symmetry
molecules.ccsd_t_source: NIST CCCBDB; HEAT-345 (Tajti et al. 2004); Karton & Martin J. Chem. Phys. 136, 124114 (2012).
molecules.notes: 10e total. Td-symmetric. (4e,4o) = t2 C-H σ frontier orbitals.

extraction_history.notes: 5 vendored bench modules added; all replay to within 84.8 µHa of in-AS CASCI(4,4) (chem-acc bound = 1600 µHa). extract script also picked up qiskit-nature 0.7.2 API drift fixes (DistanceUnit.ANGSTROM + StatevectorEstimator + hamiltonian.constants.values()).

ch4 extracted: 81.7s wall, 325 Pauli, |Δ|=0.330 µHa replay vs CASCI(4,4). hf and f2 marked NOT_AVAILABLE_STO3G: STO-3G doesn't have enough spatial orbitals for 4e/4o on these molecules (HF = 6 spatial, F2 = 10 spatial; frozen-core inactive leaves <4 active orbitals available). Would need cc-pVDZ+ basis or smaller AS.

honesty_notes: The acceptance criterion |Δ| < 1.6 mHa is vs the *same-active-space* CASCI(N,M) reference, NOT vs the published-best CCSD(T)/CBS energy. The published CCSD(T) numbers in this manifest are cited so an external reader can independently sanity-check that the qmirror VQE result is in the expected ballpark for the active space at the cited geometry; they are NOT the direct comparator.

Going from CASCI(4,4)/STO-3G to CCSD(T)/CBS typically incurs several hundred mHa of basis-set + correlation error. Those errors are properties of the chemistry choices (basis, active space), not of qmirror.

HF and F2 carry status NOT_AVAILABLE_STO3G: the 4e/4o active space cannot be constructed at STO-3G because there are too few spatial orbitals after frozen-core inactivation. This is a basis-set limitation, not a qmirror gap. Re-running with cc-pVDZ or 6-31G** would enable 4e/4o for these two; tracked but not in this bench's STO-3G scope.
Build-time only: bench/extract/extract_4e4o_hamiltonian.sh is offline tooling (per AGENTS.md §1 — same category as H2 / LiH / CMT vendored constants); the qmirror runtime is pure-hexa.
